title: Useful life must be positive
user_message: The asset's useful life must be a positive number of years.

It is a fundamental concept in accounting, and by definition, an asset cannot have a useful life of zero or a negative number of years. A positive useful life ensures that depreciation can be properly allocated over time, reflecting the asset's gradual consumption or wear and tear.
